overview: A full-stage verification pipeline designed to scan, pre-process, analyze, retrieve external evidence, and evaluate digital content (text, URLs, and screenshots) to calculate a calibrated trust score.

stages.name: Claim Extraction
stages.description: Pre-processes raw user input BEFORE any API calls are made. It normalizes text, strips noise, extracts key entities, and categorizes the claim type.
keyOperations: Strips HTML tags
Removes emojis and symbols using regex Unicode blocks
Strips forwarded message prefixes (e.g. Fwd:, share this:, breaking:, must read:)
Normalizes punctuation clutter (e.g. !!!!! becomes !)
Standardizes currency spacing (e.g. ₹ 50,000 becomes ₹50000)
Detects claim type (factual, opinion, question, or spam) based on linguistic markers and patterns
Extracts entities such as dates, currency amounts, and proper nouns
stages.input: Raw text, URL, or screenshot content

stages.name: Linguistic Risk Analysis
stages.description: Analyzes the normalized text for psychological manipulation tactics, Clickbait, urgency framing, and other stylistic markers common in misinformation.
keyOperations: Scans for Urgency signals (e.g. 'forward immediately', 'act now')
Scans for Emotional Manipulation (e.g. 'shocking', 'mind blowing', 'unbelievable')
Scans for Conspiracy / Distrust signals (e.g. 'they dont want you to know', 'wake up')
Scans for Scam patterns (e.g. 'free cash', 'claim your prize', 'guaranteed income')
Scans for Excessive Certainty phrases (e.g. '100% proven', 'undeniable proof')
Scans for Clickbait framing (e.g. 'what happened next', 'one weird trick')
Penalizes excessive capitalization and punctuation abuse
Applies trust bonuses (reduces penalties) if reputable citations are present (e.g. 'according to', 'research shows')
stages.output: LinguisticRiskResult containing safety score (0-100), risk level, and matched signals

stages.name: Parallel Evidence & Knowledge Retrieval
stages.description: Retrieves external corroborating or debunking evidence in parallel. Targets both recent breaking news (Evidence Engine) and established structured facts (Knowledge Layer).
keyOperations: Queries Google Fact Check Tools API for prior fact-checker ratings
Queries GDELT Project News API for real-time news coverage within a 6-month window
Queries Wikipedia REST API for background encyclopedic summaries
Queries Wikidata Entity Search for structured entities and statements
Queries Open Library API for bibliographic / book catalog records
Calculates token-based semantic similarity using the Jaccard index
stages.output: Independent evidence/knowledge reports with sources, reliability scores, and similarity values

stages.name: Consensus Engine
stages.description: Aggregates retrieved sources and assesses cross-source consensus. Determines whether sources agree, conflict, or if there is insufficient evidence.
keyOperations: Tiers sources (Tier 1: Government/WHO/Reuters/BBC; Tier 2: Top newspapers/fact-checkers/Wikipedia; Tier 3: Blogs/unrecognized domains)
Calculates a weighted agreement ratio based on source tiers
Boosts confidence score if multiple Tier 1/2 sources agree
Penalizes confidence and flags the claim for manual review if Tier 1 sources directly contradict each other
stages.input: Collected list of sources from Step 3
stages.output: ConsensusResult (verdict, agreement ratio, confidence boost, and manual review flag)

stages.name: Trust Score Engine
stages.description: Calculates the final explainable trust score using a weighted average of all pipeline metrics, applying overrides for special edge cases.
keyOperations: Applies scoring formula: 35% Source Reliability + 25% Evidence Agreement + 20% Semantic Match + 10% Linguistic Risk + 10% Rule Engine
Resolves mismatches: Knowledge Layer overrides Fact-Check API if fact-checkers debunked a different but related sub-claim
Overrides score to low trust (max 20) if Step 1 detected spam patterns
Generates a mathematically traceable, user-friendly explanation of the final trust score
stages.output: TrustScoreOutput containing final score, status, confidence, breakdown, and explanation

inputsDescription.sourceReliability: 0-100: Average reliability score of found sources (e.g. WHO/Reuters = 99, general news = 65-88, web search = 50)
inputsDescription.evidenceAgreement: 0-100: The consensus agreement percentage among supporting vs opposing sources
inputsDescription.semanticMatch: 0-100: The highest Jaccard token-overlap similarity between the query and retrieved source excerpts
inputsDescription.linguisticRisk: 0-100: Linguistic safety score where 100 represents calm/neutral text and low scores indicate high manipulation risk
inputsDescription.ruleEngine: 0-100: Scoring from a local preset rule database matching specific claim keywords
